methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nuclearn.ai is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Bradley Fox and Jerrold Vincent that has raised $10.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 companies across the nuclear energy sector—three public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Fuse Energy, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach, using the $10.5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $21M. The peer-median method is not applicable: while four nuclear energy peers exist in the dataset, none satisfy the threshold of three or more comparables when filtered by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier. Relying on the full-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the diversity of nuclear subsectors and funding profiles.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $18M to $24M, with a median point estimate of $21M. This range reflects a ±15% confidence band around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficiently similar-stage peer transactions to validate the 2.0x multiplier in the nuclear energy context specifically, and the limited granularity available on peer fundraise amounts and post-money valuations. The valuation should be revisited once additional Series A or Series B nuclear energy transactions become available for benchmarking.
